title: Session display Phase 3 — Detach (detachable windows)
body:
## Requirement

**Session display Phase 3 — Detach: detachable session windows.** Move a session into its own OS window (draggable to a 2nd monitor), session keeps running; reattach back into the main window.

- **Legacy stays the default** (non-breaking); detach is an **action**, not a third exclusive mode. Controlled via setting `global.sessionDisplayMode`.
- Builds on **Phase 1 tabs (#6)** + **Phase 2 settings window (#7)** (both done) — uses the multi-window pattern proven there.
- Central change: route `terminal-data` per session to the owning window (sidebar/attention events stay in the main window).

Detailed design and implementation plan → comment.

title: Review 2026-07: security hardening of local attack surface
body:
# Security hardening: local attack surface (hook server, path guards, file loads)

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** — reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. All items are local-attack-surface hardening (defense in depth for a desktop app), not remote vulnerabilities.

## Findings

- `main.js:2090` — **medium** — the attention-hook HTTP server has no authentication (no token in path or header): any local process can forge attention signals and, via `session_id`, trigger the undebounced `refreshFile(immediate)` (line 2115) → local DoS through forced full reads. Fix: random token in the hook URL, verify on POST.
- `schedule-ipc.js:191-194` — **medium** — path validation of `run-schedule-now` only checks basename `schedule-*.md` + parent dir name `commands`, not that `.claude` sits above it. A compromised renderer can execute any file `X/commands/schedule-*.md` (incl. `~/.claude/commands/`) with arbitrary frontmatter (allowed-tools, permission-mode); `projectPath` then becomes e.g. `~`. Fix: additionally require `path.basename(dotClaudeDir) === '.claude'`, optionally whitelist against known project paths.
- `main.js:1247` — **medium** — `save-plan`: `resolved.startsWith(PLANS_DIR)` without a `path.sep` boundary → `~/.claude/plansXYZ/...` passes the guard. Fix: check the `PLANS_DIR + path.sep` prefix (pattern exists in `isAllowedMemoryPath`).
- `main.js:1707` + `main.js:1727` — **low** — read/delete-work-file containment = substring `.work-files` anywhere in the path → files under arbitrary `.work-files` directories (also outside known projects) are readable/deletable. Fix: validate against known project roots.
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:649-655` — **low** — `[Image: source: <path>]` in transcript text loads arbitrary local paths via `file://` into the DOM — transcript-controlled (agent-processed third-party content). Fix: restrict to whitelisted directories / existence check.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green. The `run-schedule-now` validation should be extracted into a pure function with unit tests (see also the test-gap issue).

title: orphaned hidden projects
body:
## Requirement

**Bug: orphaned hidden projects** — `hiddenProjects` (switchboard DB setting) is **not** cleaned up when a project is removed (project management / from Claude); `get-hidden-projects` (`main.js:611`) returns the raw list without reconciling against `.claude.json`/existence → the add-project hidden list shows removed projects (verified: several already-removed projects kept showing up, none still in `.claude.json`). Fix: prune `hiddenProjects` on project remove **and** filter `get-hidden-projects` against `.claude.json`. Files: `main.js`.

title: Scope-filtered bookmark views (session / project / global)
body:
Bookmarks need scope-aware views.

**View messages bookmark list**
- Default to the **current session's** bookmarks.
- A scope switcher at the top with three levels: **Session / Project / Global**.

**Project bookmark view**
- A separate **bookmarks icon in the project toolbar** (next to the tasks icon) opens a **large bookmark view** for that project (same pattern as the task view).

Both views support jumping to the bookmarked message and removing a bookmark.

title: Review 2026-07: UX consistency — confirmations, error feedback, dialog patterns
body:
# UX consistency: confirmations, error feedback, dialog patterns

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** — reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. Goal: one consistent pattern per action class (destructive → confirm or undo; failure → visible feedback; app dialogs, never native `alert`/`confirm`).

## Findings

- `public/dialogs.js:212-217` and `:75-80` — **medium** — `launchTerminalSession`/`launchScheduleCreator`: when `openTerminal` fails, the error is written only into the (invisible) terminal and `showSession()` is not called → the user never sees the failure. `launchNewSession` (app.js:1793-1797) shows it correctly.
- `public/grid-view.js:237-241` — **medium** — bulk "Stop N running" does not mark sessions as user-stopped (`window._markUserStopped`), unlike `confirmAndStopSession` (app.js:1494) → misleading "re-click to relaunch" banner + tab auto-close for deliberately stopped sessions.
- `public/tasks-view.js:331-337` — **medium** — task delete (×) has neither confirm nor undo; archive has an undo toast, stop has a confirm dialog. At least add an undo toast.
- `public/session-tabs.js:126-133` — **medium** — tab close with `tabCloseBehavior=stopSession` kills the process without asking; the sidebar stop always confirms (`confirmAndStopSession`). Same destructive action, inconsistent pattern.
- `public/sidebar.js:1561` — **medium** — worktree-delete error uses native `alert()` — the only place; everywhere else `showControlMessage`/`showControlDialog`.
- `public/viewer-panel.js:192,199,202` + `public/variables-admin.js:323` — **medium** — native `window.confirm`/`alert` for delete confirmation/errors while the rest of the app uses control dialogs/toasts.
- `public/control-dialogs.js:111-135` + `public/style.css:250` — **medium** — toasts do not stack: simultaneous `showControlToast` calls overlap pixel-exactly (fixed bottom/right), only the top one is readable. Fix: toast container with flex stacking.
- `public/viewer-panel.js:333-345` — **medium** — `_save()` has no catch and no error feedback: if `onSave` throws or returns `ok:false`, nothing happens for the user. Fix: catch + error flash/toast; success flash only on ok.
- `public/plans-memory-view.js:242-260` — **medium** — schedule play button shows checkmark + "Launched!" before `result.ok` is checked; errors go to `console.error` only; a rejected promise leaves the button stuck in the spinner. Fix: check result first, error branch with toast + icon reset.
- `public/handoff.js:321-325` — **medium** — `handoff-del`: a `deleteHandoff` failure is swallowed (`catch {}`) and the entry removed from the list anyway → UI claims deleted although it failed; also no confirm before delete.
- `public/sidebar.js:1178-1182` — **medium** — `finalizeSidebar` focuses the active terminal after every re-render (i.e. every status tick); keyboard focus on sidebar buttons/headers is constantly stolen (a11y — defeats `makeButtonLike`). The guard only checks inputs.
- `public/bookmarks-tags.js:456-461` + `public/tasks-view.js:344` — **low** — jump/open passes a minimal `{ sessionId }` → viewer title shows a raw UUID instead of the session name; try `sessionMap.get(sessionId)` first.
- `public/handoff.js:181-196` — **low** — `showHandoffCaptureBar` has no Escape handler and no way to dismiss except the two buttons; the promise/bar stays open forever when ignored.
- `public/project-sort.js:48` — **low** — `localeCompare(…, 'de')` hardcoded although the app is English throughout; slightly wrong alpha sort for non-German users. Fix: `undefined` (system locale).
- `public/sidebar.js:1416-1421` vs. `:1736-1741` — **low** — project favorite toggle triggers a full `loadProjects()` (2× getProjects IPC) while session pin only does `refreshSidebar({resort:true})` — inconsistent and needlessly expensive.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green. UI strings in English, matching existing tone.

title: Terminal mouse mode: direct left-click selection with working TUI wheel scroll (PowerShell-style)
body:
# Feature proposal: direct left-click text selection while a TUI captures the mouse (decision pending)

Status: **proposal — not yet decided whether/how to ship.** This issue documents the problem, two candidate solutions with trade-offs, and the research references, so the decision can be made (and implemented) in a later session without re-research.

## Problem

When a TUI (Claude Code) enables xterm mouse reporting (DEC private modes 1000/1002/1003/1006), xterm.js forwards mouse events to the program:

- Left-drag no longer selects text locally — selection requires Shift+drag (Win/Linux) or Alt+drag (macOS with `macOptionClickForcesSelection`).
- The existing "Mouse reporting" setting (off = `stripMouseReporting`) restores left-click selection but kills wheel scrolling inside the TUI, because wheel reports are stripped too.
- PageUp/PageDown are keyboard input and are unaffected in every scenario.

Goal: left-click selects text directly AND the wheel still scrolls the TUI content — the classic Windows console (conhost/PowerShell QuickEdit) feel.

Upstream xterm.js offers no official option; the custom-mouse-bindings request is open since 2018 and marked out-of-scope (see references).

## Option A — patch `SelectionService.shouldForceSelection` (internal API)

Verified against the bundled `@xterm/xterm` 6.0.x: the method is

```js
shouldForceSelection(e){ return isMac ? e.altKey && macOptionClickForcesSelection : e.shiftKey }
```

Override per terminal instance in `createTerminalEntry` (public/terminal-manager.js):

```js
const svc = terminal._core._selectionService;
const orig = svc.shouldForceSelection.bind(svc);
svc.shouldForceSelection = (e) => e.button === 0 || orig(e);
```

Effect: plain left-drag always performs local selection (button events are then not reported to the program while selecting); wheel reports keep flowing to the TUI (separate path via CoreMouseService); keyboard untouched.

**Pros**
- Minimal code (~5 lines + setting).
- Wheel behavior stays 100% native (real SGR wheel reports — exact TUI semantics).
- Copy works immediately via the existing Ctrl+C-with-selection handler.

**Cons**
- `_core` is internal API — fragile across xterm upgrades; needs a guard (feature-detect the method, fall back gracefully) and a note in the upgrade checklist.
- The TUI loses left-click events entirely (Claude Code barely uses them, but e.g. clicking UI elements in other TUIs would stop working).
- Middle/right button behavior must be re-checked after the patch (right-click is already intercepted by our context menu).

## Option B — conhost/PowerShell emulation via public API

Building blocks, all public:

1. Strip mouse reporting (existing setting/`stripMouseReporting`) → left-click selects locally, exactly like conhost QuickEdit. While stripping, remember per session that the program *wanted* mouse mode (it currently just drops the sequences).
2. Right-click paste already exists (`terminalRightClick` mode `copy-paste` — PuTTY/conhost style).
3. Missing piece — wheel: `terminal.attachCustomWheelEventHandler(e => …)` (public xterm API, present in the bundled 6.0.x). When the program had requested mouse mode, translate wheel ticks into key input to the PTY (e.g. `\x1b[5~`/`\x1b[6~` PageUp/PageDown, or 3× arrow up/down per tick) so the TUI scrolls as if the user pressed keys. When no mouse mode was requested (plain shell), do nothing → wheel scrolls xterm scrollback locally as usual.

**Pros**
- Public API only — survives xterm upgrades.
- Complete conhost feel: left-click select, right-click paste, wheel scrolls content, Bild↑/↓ native.
- Conditional behavior (TUI vs. plain shell) — plain shells keep local scrollback scrolling.

**Cons**
- Emulated keys ≠ native wheel semantics: granularity must be tuned (1 tick = one PageUp is coarse; 3× arrow may be interpreted differently by the TUI — Claude Code's exact key handling needs testing).
- More code (~30-40 lines: session flag while stripping, wheel handler, setting).
- Key choice is app-specific; other TUIs may expect different scroll keys.

## Recommendation (from the review session)

Option B as the shipped mode (robust, upgrade-safe), exposed as a third mouse setting, e.g. `Mouse mode: native | select (PowerShell-style)`, default `native`. Option A only as a fallback experiment if B's key emulation feels wrong in Claude Code.

## References

- https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/issues/1536 (custom mouse bindings — out of scope upstream)
- https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/discussions/4320 (mouse reporting toggle discussion)
- https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/issues/4329 (force-selection modifier differences)
- https://xtermjs.org/docs/api/terminal/interfaces/iterminaloptions/

## Acceptance (once approved)

- New setting wired through settings-panel + live apply (pattern: `window._applyTerminalMouseReporting`).
- Left-drag selects text while Claude Code runs; wheel scrolls Claude Code's transcript; PageUp/PageDown unchanged; plain shells keep local scrollback wheel behavior.
- `npm test` green; fork-feature documented in README "What this fork adds" + docs/fork-features.md.

title: Terminal: TUI line renders doubled (ghost line), heals on resize — status + prompt line
body:
Master issue for the **doubled TUI line / ghost-line** bug (consolidates #64).

An in-place-rewritten TUI row in an active Claude session occasionally renders
**doubled**: an upper copy stays frozen while the lower copy keeps updating. A
terminal **resize heals it immediately**. Two instances of the same symptom:

- **Working-status / spinner line** (e.g. `✳ Boogieing…`) — mid-stream, no resize
  needed to trigger it. Seen on Windows / ConPTY. (was #64)
- **Input / prompt line** (`> ▌`) — the originally filed case here.

Not a fit/geometry overshoot (#59 class) — geometry is correct, only the paint is
stale. Resize heals it because it forces a full redraw (and, on ConPTY, a full frame).

**Expected:** any in-place-rewritten line repaints cleanly; a single, live-updating
line only; no manual resize needed.

Keep this issue **open until the fix is confirmed in daily use** — the bug is not
deterministically reproducible, so green tests do not verify it. Root-cause
hypotheses, the render-vs-buffer-ghost split, related refs, rollback, and known
risks live in the comments.

title: Transcript: in-viewer message search — match list, click-to-jump, term highlight, message-type filter
body:
In the message transcript viewer ("view messages"), add an in-viewer search.

**Requirement**
- **Search** the currently viewed transcript for a term.
- **List all matches** — every found occurrence shown as a result entry.
- **Click a match → jump** to that spot in the transcript (scroll/navigate to it).
- **Highlight** the search term in the matched text.
- **Filter by message type** — LLM (assistant) messages vs prompt (user) messages —
  *where the underlying transcript data supports distinguishing them* ("nur wenn möglich").

Plan/design and implementation notes go in comments.

title: Worktree/session history navigation
body:
## Requirement

**Worktree/session history navigation** _(Source: supacode)_ — Hotkey (e.g. Alt+←/→) to step forward/back through the most recently selected sessions/projects (MRU stack). Fits the porting workflow with many worktrees/branches. Pure renderer nav over the existing session switch.

title: Per-session/project color + custom title
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session/project color + custom title** _(Source: supacode)_ — freely selectable accent color per session/project row + optional short title for quick visual separation with many parallel sessions. Distinction: `customizing-colors.md` is global theming, here **per entry**. May use existing favorites/groups infrastructure.

title: "Dismiss All" for notifications
body:
## Requirement

**"Dismiss All" for notifications** _(Source: supacode)_ — a button/action that acknowledges all open in-app notifications / attention markers at once. Complements the Attention inbox (#13). Small, pure renderer + possibly state reset.

title: Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)
body:
## Requirement

**Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)** _(Source: supacode)_ — open a repo/project on a remote host via SSH and manage it in Switchboard like a local one: session scan, terminal (PTY over SSH), file/diff access. **Large** — needs its own detailed plan (transport, auth via Bitwarden SSH agent, remote `~/.claude/projects` scan, PTY bridging, watcher replacement). Clarify scope/architecture before implementation.

title: Agent badges: which tool runs where (evaluate)
body:
## Requirement

**Agent badges: which tool runs where (evaluate)** _(source: supacode)_ — per session/row a badge showing the **agent type** (Claude / Codex / Copilot CLI / OpenCode) + live presence, hook-driven. Partial redundancy with #13 (status chips, but Claude only). Only makes sense together with #42 (Multi-LLM). **First evaluate** whether standalone or folded into #13.

title: Multi-LLM / multi-agent support (Codex, …)
body:
## Requirement

**Multi-LLM / multi-agent support (Codex, …)** — alongside Claude Code, launch & manage other coding CLIs as a session type: **Codex**, GitHub Copilot CLI, OpenCode, and others. Affects session start (command/args per agent), detection/scan (not just `~/.claude/projects`), possibly a dedicated session-cache source. **Large**, needs its own detailed plan; foundation for #41.

title: Detect subagent spawn via .meta.json (not only .jsonl)
body:
## Requirement

**Detect subagent spawn via `.meta.json` (not only `.jsonl`)** — when spawning a task subagent, Claude Code immediately writes `<parent>/subagents/agent-<id>.meta.json` (`agentType`, `description`, `toolUseId`, `spawnDepth`), but the `.jsonl` transcript only once the agent produces output. A spawned agent that did not (yet) run to producing output (queued, parent turn ends/is interrupted) has **only** `meta.json` → `detectSubagentTransitions`/`enumerateSessionFiles` (which only scan `subagents/*.jsonl`) never see it (observed 2026-07-02: session `876c2b28`, "Feldtexte W6" agents — meta identical to runnable W5 agents, but without jsonl; parent last at 22:26, spawns at 22:28). **No special background-agents format, no data loss — pure visibility.** Fix: also scan `subagents/*.meta.json` → placeholder subagent row (name from `description`, status "spawned/empty/aborted") as soon as the meta is there; upgrade to a full row when the `.jsonl` arrives. Files: `read-session-file.js` (`enumerateSessionFiles`), `session-transitions.js`, `session-cache.js`, subagent rendering in `public/sidebar.js`.

title: Grid layout a11y + DOM tests
body:
## Requirement

**Grid layout a11y + DOM tests** — bonus addition to #04 (code done, beyond the Spec-08 acceptance criteria). (a) **Keyboard operation** for card resize/reorder: currently only pointer drag (`startCardResize`/`startCardDrag`), no keyboard path; `navigateGrid` only moves focus, the resize handle is `aria-hidden`. To add: resize the focused card via `Shift`+arrow, a "move mode" for reordering, ARIA live announcements. File: `public/grid-view.js`. (b) **DOM/integration tests** for resize/drag/persistence/group-drop — so far only the pure model is tested (`test/grid-layout.test.js`), the interaction path is untested; needs a jsdom or Electron E2E harness (the project runs `node --test` without a DOM).

title: HTML/MD preview panel (+ image preview)
body: ## Requirement **HTML/MD preview panel (+ image preview)** _(source: brianstanley)_ — show rendered Markdown/HTML of a file as a preview. Renderer-side, reusing the existing `marked` + tag escape/sanitizing from the JSONL viewer. Ref: `brianstanley/main` @ `bbc9e52`. **When porting, watch out for:** (a) preserve the #46 DOMPurify wrap at both Markdown-innerHTML spots (the brianstanley diff writes raw `marked` back at `viewer-panel.js:397`); (b) sign off on HTML preview = sandboxed iframe (`allow-same-origin`, no `allow-scripts`). **Additional scope (from #48 test):** the file panel/`ViewerPanel` currently renders images as byte text (`read-file-for-panel` always reads UTF-8) — add image extensions (`png/jpg/jpeg/gif/webp/svg`) as ``/data-URL preview; possibly a binary read path in the IPC. Files: `public/{viewer-panel,viewer-toolbar,file-panel}.js`, `style.css`, possibly `main.js`/`preload.js`.

title: Per-session API key override
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session API key override** _(split off from #47)_ — optional password field in the session dialogs that injects `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` **only transiently** per session into the PTY `env` (`open-terminal`, `main.js`) — **never** in the command string or the settings DB. Persistence only via `saved_variables` `secret` (`safeStorage`). Picker layer from `brianstanley` @ `fb81d18`. **Net-new** (we are OAuth/Claude-only) — before implementing, clarify whether it's even wanted.

title: Open files in a configurable external editor
body:
Let users open files in their own external editor.

- A **configurable external editor** (command / path) in settings.
- In the integrated file panel, a toolbar button next to "Toggle line wrapping" that opens the current file in the external editor and **closes the panel**.
- In the terminal, **Ctrl/Cmd+click** on a file link opens it in the external editor; a plain click keeps opening the integrated panel.
- Fall back to the OS default application when no external editor is configured.

title: Review 2026-07: code consolidation — duplicated logic and dead code
body:
# Code consolidation: duplicated logic and dead code

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** — reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. Pure refactoring: no behavior change, each consolidation must keep tests green.

## Duplications

- `claude-config.js:74-189` — `setProjectTrust` / `removeProjectEntry` / `renameProjectEntry`: 3× identical read→parse→`.bak`→tmp→rename block → shared `mutateClaudeConfig(fn)`.
- `main.js:1977` / `:2566` / `:2253` — settings cascade (default→global→project) written out 3× (resolveShellTypeForProject, open-terminal IIFE, get-effective-settings) → `effectiveSetting(key, projectPath)` helper.
- `main.js:2427` vs. `:2444` — read-session-jsonl / read-subagent-jsonl: identical "file→entries[]" parse block → shared `readJsonlEntries(path)`.
- `public/dialogs.js:235-327` vs. `:386-461` — permission-mode grid (modes array, `renderModeGrid`, grid click handler, overlay/key wiring) duplicated 1:1 between New and Resume dialog → shared `buildPermissionModeDialog()`.
- `public/dialogs.js:62-69`, `:196-203`, `public/app.js:1773-1780` — "inject pending session into cachedProjects/cachedAllProjects" block 3× → `injectPendingSession(session, projectPath)`.
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:724-733`/`819-829`/`333-341` (toolResultMap) and `:757-767`/`:848-858`/`:495-503` (fullscreen overlay) — transcript render logic duplicated 3× between `showJsonlViewer`, `showSubagentTranscript`, agent expand → shared `renderTranscript(entries, container)`.
- `public/sidebar.js:2386-2479` vs. `:2484-2553` — drag scaffold (threshold, ghost, moveGhost, cleanup, onUp) copied twice (session drag vs. project drag) → generic `startPointerDrag(opts)`.
- `public/app.js:2101-2175` — tab switch hides viewers per branch with 7+ hand-maintained `style.display='none'` lists (already diverged between branches) → `hideAllViewers()` + show target explicitly.
- `public/app.js:2512-2519` vs. `public/settings-panel.js:1191-1195` — usage-threshold clamping implemented twice with identical semantics → one helper in utils.
- `workers/search-query.js:64,78-80` vs. `db.js:370,1040-1042` — `FTS_QUERY_MAX_CHARS` + escape/`title:` logic duplicated ("must match" comment instead of a shared module) → shared `search-query-util.js`.
- `shell-profiles.js:40-44` vs. `:128-133` — Git-Bash candidate list maintained twice → shared constant.
- `workers/scan-projects.js:10-23` vs. `session-cache.js:48-70` — `readFolderFromFilesystem` (deriveProjectPath → enumerate → readSessionFile) exists twice; drift risk → shared Electron-free helper.
- `public/codemirror-setup.js:56-68` vs. `:109-117` — identical match-scan loop in `searchHighlighter` and `findAll()` → shared `findMatches(doc, term)`.
- `public/terminal-manager.js:689-704` vs. `:727-734` — `linkHandler.activate` and the WebLinksAddon callback carry the same logic (button guard + `file://` branch) → one shared `activateLink(event, uri)`.

## Dead code / cleanup

- `read-session-file.js:336` — `readSessionDisplayHeader` is unused (see also the sync-IO issue, where it should become used again — coordinate).
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:14-52`, `:285-301` — dead live-tail infrastructure: `liveSubagents` only ever filled, `data-subagent-watch-key` never set, `activeWatchId` never assigned. Remove or finish the feature.
- `sqlite-busy-retry.js:22` — unreachable `throw lastErr` (last attempt rethrows in the catch) → remove.
- `derive-project-path.js:47` — signature takes 1 argument but ≥6 call sites pass a second `folder` (main.js:873,1455,1638, session-cache.js:51,449,569, workers/scan-projects.js:12) — dead argument suggests unused semantics → clean call sites.
- `public/plans-memory-view.js:70-76` — active marking via text comparison of all `.session-id` (breaks with same-named files) while `openMemory:273` correctly uses `data-filepath` + `CSS.escape` → same data-attribute technique.
- `public/style.css` — ~15 top-level selectors defined more than once (e.g. `.variable-secret-pill` 7664+7681, `.session-stop-btn` 2007+2071, `.grid-card` 2×); complementary today but collision-prone → consolidate per selector.

## Acceptance

- Each item consolidated or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green; zero behavior change (pure refactor).

title: Review 2026-07: test gaps and build-script hardening
body:
# Test gaps and build-script hardening

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing.**

## Test gaps

- `schedule-ipc.js` — **medium** — entirely untested; especially the security-relevant `run-schedule-now` path validation (see the security-hardening issue) is pure logic and trivially testable once extracted into a function.
- `shell-profiles.js` — **medium** — `test/schedule-injection.test.js` covers only bash+pwsh quoting; the (buggy, see robustness issue) cmd branch, `resolveShell`, and `shellArgs` are untested.
- `encode-project-path.js` — **low** — no test although the module must reproduce CLI behavior ("reverse-engineered from claude CLI 2.1.126"); the >200-char hash branch is regression-prone.
- `test/folder-index-state.test.js:18` — **low** — real 1.1 s sleep to advance mtime — slows the suite. Fix: `fs.utimesSync` with an explicit timestamp.
- `workers/scan-projects.js` — **low** — no protocol test (progress/final/error message shape) although the search worker has one (`search-worker-protocol.test.js`); shape drift vs. the consumer would go unnoticed.

## Build scripts

- `scripts/generate-icons.js:117-131` — **low** — final async IIFE without `.catch` → on `loadImage` failure an unhandled rejection instead of exit code ≠ 0 (build "succeeds" without Linux icons). Fix: `.catch(e => { console.error(e); process.exit(1); })`.
- `scripts/postinstall.js:28` + `scripts/generate-icons.js:63-72` — **low** — `execSync` with string-interpolated paths (project convention is `execFile`); breaks on paths with special chars; PIL is an undeclared build dependency for generate-icons. Fix: `execFileSync` with arg arrays; document the PIL requirement.
- `scripts/build-backlog.js:21` — **low** — `--limit 200`: with >200 open issues the mirror silently truncates. Fix: warn/fail at `issues.length === 200` or paginate.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- New tests follow the existing `node --test` style in `test/`; `npm test` green.

title: Make the terminal LRU cap configurable
body:
The renderer keeps at most 12 live xterm instances (`TERMINAL_LRU_CAP` in `public/terminal-manager.js`); the least-recently-shown closed session beyond the cap is destroyed (soft cap: running and active sessions are never evicted).

Since WebGL is the default renderer, the cap also serves as the GL-context budget (Chromium allows ~16 per process; a lost context falls back to the DOM renderer).

Expose the cap as a setting for users with many parallel sessions and enough RAM. Constraints: keep 12 as the default; values above ~14 should hint that terminals beyond the GL budget silently fall back to the DOM renderer.

Deferred from #81 (was rejected there as "defer until the cap is hit").
